mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.5% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 2.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate earnings compression.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces forward earnings expectations. When both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month simultaneously, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of co-decline instances, or the interaction coefficient between RSAFS and HOUST changes sign across rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 105.0 (index 2017=100) signals robust supply-side capacity utilization that supports corporate profit margins and enables positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days, conditional on inflation remaining below 3.5% year-over-year.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO and returns becomes negative when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerating to below 100,000 net new jobs per month signals labor market slack that reduces wage pressure and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as inflation expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < 100,000 for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of deceleration instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured at quarterly frequency above 2.5% annualized signals sustained economic expansion that supports equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors maintain risk-on positioning.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 45% of expansion periods (GDPC1 > 2.5% annualized), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 4.0% annualized, the real policy rate is restrictive and signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of restrictive instances, or the interaction effect between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL becomes insignificant (p > 0.10) in rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declining month-over-month while unemployment rises simultaneously signals a supply shock combined with demand weakness that triggers equity selloffs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice recession risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of co-deterioration instances, or the joint probability of negative returns given both conditions is not significantly higher than marginal probabilities (chi-square test p > 0.05).
